item.title: Adversarial examples
item.description:
As deep learning (DL) being applied in many safety-critical environments, the reliability and rebustness of DL models are highly desired. However, the recent emergence of adversarial examples posed great concerns of the confidence in even state of the art DL models. In this post, let’s see some methods of generating and defensing adversarial examples.

An adversarial example is a sample of input data which has been modified very slightly in a way that the modification are imperceptible to human but can easily fool deep neural networks.

Reference
